data.description: Explore and produce a plan document at docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md for a unified theme engine that covers all c11mux chrome surfaces (sidebar, top bar, tab bar, dividers, outer workspace frame) while leaving Ghostty-owned surfaces untouched. Plan must cover current-state audit, workspace color audit, schema design, divider/frame primitives, migration, UX, phased rollout, non-goals, open questions, risks.
data.title: Custom theming system plan

data.body: Plan document drafted at docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md (1085 lines). Covers all 10 sections from the prompt: current-state audit, workspace color audit, C11muxTheme schema (TOML), divider + frame primitives, migration story, discoverability/UX, phased rollout (M1-M5), non-goals, open questions for human review, risks. Includes 3 built-in theme examples in Appendix A.

7 open questions locked 2026-04-18 before Trident review:
1. Default theme = Stage 11 brand (void-dominant, gold accent)
2. Frame thickness = 1.5pt
3. Inactive-frame opacity = 0.25
4. Tab-bar active indicator default = $workspaceColor
5. CLI namespace: `cmux themes` stays Ghostty (king theme); c11mux chrome under `cmux ui themes`
6. Per-workspace theme-file override deferred past v1 (M5 if asked)
7. TOML parser: hand-written subset (~200 lines, zero deps)

Plan updated to v1.1. Trident plan review command ready to issue next.

Trident plan review complete. ~50 actionable findings across the 3 synthesis docs: 32 accepted (folded into plan), 8 rejected (recorded in §13.11 with rationale), 10 escalated to new §14 'Open questions post-Trident'. Review pack at docs/c11mux-theming-plan-review-pack-2026-04-18T2026/ (9 reviews + 3 syntheses). Plan bumped to v2. Highest-impact change: M1 split into M1a (engine + parser + default theme, no call-sites) / M1b (surface-by-surface adoption with 24-dim sidebar snapshot test) and M2 split into M2a (bonsplit-only) / M2b (wire dividers) / M2c (frame + sidebar overlay). Runtime-contract appendix added at §3 / §6.4.a / §6.5 / §8.1.a covering cache-key dimensions, cycle detection, invalid-value policy, precedence matrix, runtime-toggle split. §12 locked decisions untouched. §14 items needing your call: audience declaration (internal vs public), workspace-frame decorative-vs-structural scope, brand-review sign-off artifact, light-mode variant strategy, single-operator fallback.

provenance.reason: Rework scope: plan is locked at v2.1 (302105c8); ticket now anchors full delivery, not just the planning spike

data.from: Explore and produce a plan document at docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md for a unified theme engine that covers all c11mux chrome surfaces (sidebar, top bar, tab bar, dividers, outer workspace frame) while leaving Ghostty-owned surfaces untouched. Plan must cover current-state audit, workspace color audit, schema design, divider/frame primitives, migration, UX, phased rollout, non-goals, open questions, risks.
data.to:
Deliver a unified, TOML-schema-driven theme engine for c11mux chrome surfaces (sidebar, top bar, tab bar, pane dividers, outer workspace frame, pane title bars), while leaving Ghostty-owned surfaces (terminal cells, cursor, prompt) untouched.

Plan source of truth: docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md (v2.1 @ commit 302105c8).

Scope highlights (§12 locks, 16 items):
- Public, outward-facing utility (§12 #8) — held to public-product polish on error UX, CLI ergonomics, docs, localization.
- Two built-ins ship: stage11 (brand default, void-dominant + gold accent) and phosphor (subtle matrix/CRT-phosphor aesthetic, validates theme-switching + demonstrates aesthetic range) (§12 #15).
- Workspace frame is a structural primitive with per-surface addressable state and animation-ready rendering (§12 #9). M2c ships decorative baseline; M5+ hooks agent state, drop zones, mode indicators.
- $workspaceColor stays a pure color token. Categorical state (environment, risk, mode, tags) flows through a sibling workspaceState channel on ThemeContext — reserved in v1, implemented in v1.x (§12 #10).
- Themes are mode-agnostic; light/dark binding lives one level up as two @AppStorage slots an operator binds to any installed theme (§12 #12).
- Agent socket access: read-only theme methods; agents signal workspace state via metadata, never change the active theme (§12 #13).
- Per-bundle-ID @AppStorage isolation; shared themes directory (§12 #14).
- Hand-written TOML subset parser, zero deps (§12 #7).
- Explicit theme inheritance (`[identity].inherits`) reserved but deferred to M5+ (§12 #16).
- No formal process gates; normal operator workflow (§12 #11).

Milestone rollout (§10 of plan):
- M1a — engine + parser + default theme (no call-sites)
- M1b — surface-by-surface adoption with 24-dim sidebar snapshot test
- M2a — bonsplit DividerStyle sibling struct (submodule)
- M2b — c11mux wires dividers through theme
- M2c — outer workspace frame + sidebar overlay
- M3 — user themes dir + hot reload + phosphor ships
- M4 — Settings picker (two slots) + CLI (cmux ui themes *)
- M5 — stretch; evaluated after M4

Per cmux forward-only Lattice policy, milestone child tickets are created only when each milestone's work actually starts. This ticket is the parent anchor.

provenance.reason: Rescope description to match v2.1 plan; reflect full-delivery scope

data.body: Per 2026-04-20 operator call, reshaped children into 4 total (anchor + M1 + M2 bundle + M3+M4 bundle). M5 intentionally not ticketed — re-justify only if pursued. CMUX-33 (standalone M3) cancelled; replaced by CMUX-35.

data.body:
Anchor close-out. All three child milestones shipped on main; this parent was just left at planned.

- CMUX-21 (M1 — engine + parser + default theme + surface adoption): done. PR #28.
- CMUX-32 (M2 — workspace color prevalence + frame + dividers): done. PR #30 + C11-8 PR #45 chrome refinement.
- CMUX-35 (M3+M4 — user themes + hot reload + Settings + CLI): done. PR #31 + Radical theme PR #35 + upstream theme-picker polish.

Verified on main:
- Sources/Theme/ holds the full engine (C11muxTheme, ThemeManager, ThemedValueAST/Parser/Evaluator, TomlSubsetParser, ThemeContext, ThemeRoleRegistry, ThemeDirectoryWatcher, ThemeCanonicalizer, ThemeSocketMethods, WorkspaceFrame, ResolvedThemeSnapshot).
- Resources/c11-themes/ ships stage11 + phosphor + radical built-ins plus README.
- c11 themes CLI is live (list/get/set/clear/reload/path/dump/validate/diff).
- Light/dark slot binding via @AppStorage two-slot model, themes mode-agnostic per §12 #12.
- Per-bundle-ID @AppStorage isolation per §12 #14.
- Read-only theme socket methods per §12 #13.

§12 locks honored. M5 (stretch) intentionally not pursued, no re-justification triggered.
